hot_topics.topic: US bans Polestar EVs over Chinese ties
hot_topics.synthesis: The US Commerce Department has banned the import of new Polestar electric vehicles from model year 2027 onward, citing the company's Chinese links as part of a rule against connected cars from Chinese automakers. This decision prevents future Polestar models from being sold in the US, despite its former parent company Volvo receiving authorization. Both Ars Technica and TechCrunch reports confirm this action by the Trump administration.

hot_topics.topic: Apple increases Mac and iPad prices due to memory chip costs
hot_topics.synthesis: Apple has raised prices across much of its Mac and iPad product lineup, attributing the increases to the rising cost of memory chips. While iPhones are spared for now, consumers are advised to take advantage of existing Prime Day discounts before all retail prices reflect these hikes. The price adjustments impact various models, making current sales even more significant.

hot_topics.topic: IBM announces breakthrough sub-1 nanometer chip design
hot_topics.synthesis: IBM has announced a significant advancement in chip technology, claiming the world's first sub-1 nanometer chip design, which it calls 'nanostack' or 'block of flats.' This innovation promises nearly double the transistor density, enabling significantly more powerful and energy-efficient AI data center chips. While a major breakthrough, the technology is still some time away from production.

hot_topics.topic: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil custom AI inference chip
hot_topics.synthesis: OpenAI and Broadcom have collaborated to announce Jalapeño, their first custom chip specifically designed for large language model inference at scale in data centers. This partnership aims to optimize hardware for AI operations, marking the initial phase of a long-term development project for specialized AI silicon.

hot_topics.topic: Slate Auto's minimalist EV truck garners attention
hot_topics.synthesis: Startup Slate Auto has launched an ultra-minimalist electric pickup truck, priced under $25,000, which has generated considerable buzz for its 'underpromise, overdeliver' strategy. The company has already exceeded initial specifications for range and towing, and aims to offer an affordable EV by stripping away non-essential features, raising questions about consumer reception to such extreme simplification.

hot_topics.topic: Devastating earthquakes strike Venezuela, causing panic and deaths
hot_topics.synthesis: Venezuela has been hit by two major earthquakes, causing widespread panic, flattening buildings in Caracas, and resulting in at least 164 deaths and hundreds of injuries. The disaster is a significant blow to a country already facing political and economic uncertainty. Videos captured the moments of impact, showing frantic evacuations and the immediate aftermath.

hot_topics.topic: Anthropic alleges Alibaba illicitly extracted Claude AI model capabilities
hot_topics.synthesis: AI firm Anthropic has accused its Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting capabilities and data from its Claude AI model. Anthropic alleges that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to gain unauthorized access, raising significant concerns about intellectual property theft in the competitive AI industry.

hot_topics.topic: Trump administration faces challenges and controversy over policy decisions
hot_topics.synthesis: The Trump administration is navigating several contentious policy issues, including a request for billions for the Iran war that faces Republican opposition. Federal judges have blocked parts of Trump's order to limit voting by mail, and he unilaterally canceled the signing of a major bipartisan housing bill, effectively holding it hostage. These actions highlight ongoing political and legal battles surrounding the administration's agenda.

hot_topics.topic: Supreme Court expands government's power over immigration and asylum
hot_topics.synthesis: The Supreme Court has issued rulings that expand the government's authority over immigration, affirming the president's unreviewable power to end Temporary Protected Status programs, allowing deportations of Syrian and Haitian TPS holders. Additionally, the court ruled that federal law permits the US to prevent asylum seekers from physically entering the country, effectively blocking their ability to apply for asylum. These decisions significantly impact immigration policy and rights.

hot_topics.topic: Progressives achieve significant primary victories, alarming centrist Democrats
hot_topics.synthesis: Progressive candidates have secured a series of major victories in Democratic primaries, particularly in New York, where Democratic Socialists of America-backed candidates swept crucial races. This winning streak has alarmed moderate Democrats who fear losing the party's ideological direction and jeopardizing general election chances. The debate now focuses on whether these progressive gains will reshape the Democratic Party or create vulnerabilities in swing states.

items.title: Who are the fire-tamers?
items.summary: This article explores the hidden world of "fire-tamers" in modern France, healers who practice in parallel with conventional medicine from farmhouses to oncology clinics. Their secret methods offer an alternative approach to healing.

items.title: Apple ratchets up prices, blames the cost of memory
items.summary: Apple bumped its prices across much of its product lineup today, in some cases adding hundreds of dollars to the cost of a new Macintosh. An entry-level MacBook Neo that cost $599 is now $699. The formerly $1,299 iMac will now be a $1,499 iMac. An M5 MacBook Pro that was $1,699 i…

items.title: Disney agreed to $50M settlement over claims it made live-TV streaming expensive
items.summary: Disney settled a lawsuit for $50 million with YouTube TV and DirecTV subscribers who claimed the company forced price increases for live-TV streaming. The class-action complaint alleged Disney used anticompetitive agreements, mandating ESPN's inclusion in base packages.

items.title: Elon Musk denies Tesla’s Autopilot caused crash that killed grandmother
items.summary: Following a fatal crash where a Tesla Model 3 struck a home, killing a grandmother, the family is suing Tesla and the driver, who claims Autopilot was active. Elon Musk denies Autopilot's involvement, and police are still investigating the use of the automated driver-assist feature.

items.title: Every Homo naledi we know of is female, and the implications are fascinating
items.summary: All Homo naledi skeletons found in South Africa's Rising Star Cave are confirmed to be female, based on proteins in their dental enamel. This discovery offers a significant new piece of evidence in the ongoing debate about how these ancient hominins' remains came to be in the caves.

items.title: FCC plans ID mandate that could block anonymous use of prepaid burner phones
items.summary: A Federal Communications Commission proposal to collect more identifying information from phone users has drawn protests from privacy-focused groups and advocates for domestic violence survivors. The plan is ostensibly designed to thwart robocallers but could make it difficult fo…

items.title: Feds deny Polestar authorization to sell cars in US from model year 2027
items.summary:
The electric car brand Polestar's days in the US are seriously numbered. Today, the company revealed that the US Commerce Department has declined to authorize imports of new Polestars from model year 2027 onward as part of a rule
banning connected cars
from automakers with Chines…

items.title: Formula E reveals first calendar for GEN4 with lots of real race tracks
items.summary: Formula E unveiled its 2026–2027 season 12 calendar, incorporating many traditional race tracks like Circuit of the Americas for its new, more powerful GEN4 car. The 21-race lineup across 13 cities reflects the higher speeds the upgraded electric vehicles will achieve.

items.title: Google starts lowering Play Store fees, making good on Epic Games settlement
items.summary: Google is beginning to reduce Play Store developer fees and introduce new payment options, fulfilling its settlement agreement with Epic Games. This change follows a legal dispute where Epic alleged Google's app store policies were anticompetitive.

items.title: Hotly anticipated Grand Theft Auto VI will cost more than other AAA games
items.summary: The highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto VI will launch at an $80 price point, exceeding the current $70 standard for AAA games. Furthermore, its physical release will consist solely of a download code, rather than a physical disc.

items.title: IBM claims world’s first sub-1 nanometer chip technology
items.summary: A new chip architecture from IBM can integrate nearly 100 billion transistors on a chip the size of a human fingernail—nearly twice the transistor density of the company’s previous generation of chip technology. The resulting improvement in chip compute performance and energy eff…

items.title: One-two punch delivered in global operation disrupts cybercrime "assembly line"
items.summary: A global operation by international authorities and private tech companies disrupted a cybercrime 'assembly line' by simultaneously targeting the Amadey and StealC malware platforms. This action thwarted the collection of millions of login credentials and the theft of over $47 million.

items.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
items.summary:
OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT and Codex and the models those tools use, and Broadcom, an established silicon supplier, have
announced
a new chip, called Jalapeño, designed specifically for large language model inference in data centers.
The companies intend to deploy the chi…

items.title: Underpromise, overdeliver? Hands-on with the $24,950 Slate auto.
items.summary:
Slate provided flights from San Francisco to Los Angeles and accommodation so Ars could ride in the Slate truck. Ars does not accept paid editorial content.
LOS ANGELES—Slate Auto has pulled a Disneyland. Let me explain.
At Disneyland, if a sign for a ride says the wait is 45 min…

items.title: 'I thought I was going to die' - Venezuelans describe earthquake panic
items.summary: Buildings were flattened in the capital Caracas, where voices have been heard calling from the rubble.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
items.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

items.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
items.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

items.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
items.summary: Families in Kenya laid flowers on a barbed wire barricade to commemorate the anniversary of deadly protests, demanding justice for dozens killed during demonstrations in 2024 and the preceding year. The somber event underscored ongoing calls for accountability.

items.title: Find out which university degrees could earn you most across your lifetime
items.summary: New data reveals which university degrees offer the highest and lowest financial returns over an individual's lifetime. This analysis provides insights into the long-term earning potential associated with different academic paths.

items.title: How to cope in a heatwave - according to you
items.summary: This article compiles reader-submitted tips for managing a heatwave, covering advice from appropriate clothing to achieving restful sleep. It offers practical suggestions for staying cool during hot weather.

items.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
items.summary: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.

items.title: Independent MPs launch new Australian centrist party
items.summary: Independent Australian MPs Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender have formed a new centrist party, Community Strong Australia, aiming to promote "reason over rage" in national politics. This initiative seeks to offer a balanced alternative to existing political discourse.

items.title: Key figure in South Africa police corruption scandal pleads guilty
items.summary: Vusimusi "Cat" Matlala, a central figure in South Africa's police corruption scandal, has pleaded guilty and is expected to provide evidence against high-ranking officials. His cooperation could lead to further revelations in the widespread corruption probe.

items.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
items.summary: Oil prices have fallen back to their levels before the Iran war, a decrease largely influenced by signs of traffic gradually resuming through the crucial Strait of Hormuz shipping route. This indicates a stabilization in the global oil market.

items.title: Philippines bans video game played by alleged high school shooter
items.summary: The Philippines has banned a video game linked to an alleged high school shooter, following a rare school shooting that left three students dead and 20 injured. This action comes amid national shock over the incident.

items.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
items.summary: Ryanair announced it will reluctantly cease charging parents an £8 fee to sit next to their young children on flights. This policy change reverses a previous practice of charging per-way for family seating.

items.title: The ICE raids are over, but some Minnesotans say they still live in fear
items.summary: Despite the official end of the Trump administration's immigration crackdown, many Minnesotans, especially immigrants, continue to live in fear. The psychological and social impact of the past ICE raids persists in communities like Minneapolis.

items.title: The woman who fought for Pakistan's disappeared men now faces life in jail
items.summary: A Nobel Peace Prize nominee, known for advocating for Pakistan's disappeared men in Balochistan, is now facing a potential life sentence in jail. Her long-standing human rights activism has put her at severe personal risk.

items.title: This Indian state is trying to ensure no one grows old alone
items.summary: The Indian state of Kerala is developing new strategies to ensure its aging population does not grow old alone, particularly as migration leads to more elderly living in isolation. These efforts focus on enhancing care and support for seniors.

items.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
items.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

items.title: Watch: Moment earthquakes hit airport and buildings in Caracas
items.summary: Video filmed by BBC reporters, contributors and locals shows the aftermath, as frantic residents evacuate.

items.title: Blogging can just be stating the obvious
items.summary: This article posits that blogging can be effective by merely stating the obvious, suggesting that clarity and directness can be valuable even without groundbreaking revelations. It challenges the notion that every blog post needs to offer novel insights.

items.title: Cloudflare launched self-managed OAuth for all
items.summary: Cloudflare has rolled out self-managed OAuth functionality, making it available to all users. This new feature provides greater control and flexibility over authentication processes.

items.title: Ford rehires 350 engineers after AI fails to preserve expertise or train juniors
items.summary: Ford has rehired 350 quality inspectors because artificial intelligence systems proved insufficient at preserving expertise or training junior employees. This decision highlights a limitation of current AI applications in retaining specialized human knowledge.

items.title: Half-Life 2 in a Browser
items.summary: The popular video game Half-Life 2 has been successfully ported to run within a web browser, offering a new way to access the classic title. This achievement demonstrates advancements in web-based gaming technology.

items.title: LastPass notifies users of yet another data breach
items.summary: LastPass has issued another notification to its users regarding a new data breach. This marks a recurring security incident for the password management service.

items.title: LuaJIT 3.0 proposed syntax extensions
items.summary: Proposed syntax extensions for LuaJIT 3.0 are currently under discussion, outlining potential new features and changes for the Just-In-Time compiler. These proposals aim to enhance the language's capabilities and expressiveness.

items.title: RubyLLM: A Ruby framework for all major AI providers
items.summary: RubyLLM is a new Ruby framework designed to integrate with all major AI providers, simplifying the development of AI-powered applications in Ruby. This framework aims to offer a unified interface for various large language models.

items.title: Show HN: I made Google Trends for Hacker News by indexing 18 years of comments
items.summary: A new tool, "Google Trends for Hacker News," has been created by indexing 18 years of comments to show popular topics over time. This project aims to provide insights into historical trends within the Hacker News community.

items.title: Wikipedia Workers in Britain set global first by seeking union recognition
items.summary: Wikipedia workers in Britain are pursuing union recognition, an unprecedented move globally for the online encyclopedia's workforce. This effort aims to establish collective bargaining rights for employees in the UK.

items.title: Repositioning retail for the AI era
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is fundamentally transforming retail by embedding "AI-first" approaches into core operational systems rather than just adding superficial features. Macy's, for instance, is using AI to accelerate decision-making, optimize supply chains, and create more personalized customer experiences.

items.title: 'They can kill you': Immigrants fear a surge in xenophobic violence in South Africa
items.summary: Immigrants in South Africa are increasingly fearful of xenophobic violence, evidenced by attacks on foreign-owned businesses and migrant deaths. A prominent xenophobic group has given undocumented immigrants until June 30 to leave the country.

items.title: 2 earthquakes in Venezuela kill at least 164. And, Trump cancels housing bill signing
items.summary:
Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.
(Image credit: Manaure Quintero)

items.title: A federal judge in Boston has blocked parts of Trump's order to limit voting by mail
items.summary:
A federal judge in Boston has blocked parts of President Trump's executive order to limit voting by mail. The Trump administration is expected to appeal the ruling.
(Image credit: Alex Wong)

items.title: Supreme Court bars 'vampire rules' on gun ownership
items.summary: In a 6-3 ideologically divided decision, the Supreme Court struck down "vampire rules" that required advance permission for gun ownership, ruling them an undue burden. This decision upholds the right to possess and carry a firearm without such restrictive prerequisites.

items.title: Supreme Court says U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border
items.summary:
By a 6 to 3 vote, the high court ruled that that federal law allows the government to stop asylum seekers from physically setting foot in the United States, effectively keeping them from applying for asylum.
(Image credit: Drew Angerer)

items.title: The Viking chant that became Norway's World Cup rallying cry
items.summary: Norway's return to the World Cup is being energized by a viral Viking "rowing" chant that has gained international attention, from Times Square to Norway's parliament. This rallying cry has become a significant part of the team's anticipated performance.

items.title: Trump can begin deportations of Syrian, Haitian TPS holders, Supreme Court says
items.summary:
Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.
(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)

items.title: U.S. Supreme Court backs Monsanto in its fight against liability from popular weed killer
items.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court supported Monsanto in a case concerning liability from its Roundup weed killer, addressing whether federal law preempts state claims about pesticide labeling. This decision clarifies the legal framework for product labeling and corporate responsibility.

items.title: Centrist Democrats are freaking out about progressives’ winning streak
items.summary:
Moderate Democrats are sounding the alarm after massive losses in New York's primaries. They fear they’re on the verge of losing the party’s ideological civil war — and hurting its electoral chances.
Leftist candidates swept a trio of deep-blue House seats in New York City, a sei…

items.title: What Is the Positive Grassmannian and Why Does It Show Up Everywhere?
items.summary: The positive Grassmannian is a mathematical concept that surprisingly connects disparate phenomena like traffic flow, shallow-water waves, and quantum particle scattering. This "shape that classifies other shapes" offers a unifying framework for understanding diverse physical models.

items.title: Quoting Tom MacWright
items.summary: Tom MacWright highlights a growing trend of job applications, portfolios, and even GitHub projects being entirely generated by Large Language Models, leading to a concern about candidate authenticity. He notes that such "perfected, generated" content reveals nothing personal about the individual, beyond their use of AI tools.

items.title: simonw/browser-compat-db
items.summary: Simon Willison developed a tool to convert Mozilla's extensive browser compatibility data into a SQLite database, which is hosted on GitHub and accessible via open CORS headers. The conversion script and GitHub Actions workflow used for this project were generated with AI assistance.

items.title: Congress passed the biggest housing bill in a generation. Trump is holding it hostage.

Senate Majority Leader John Thune watches as President Donald Trump speaks to the media as the two arrive at the U.S. Capitol on Wednesday. Trump is visiting Capitol Hill to push his SAVE America Act. (Photo by
Kevin Dietsch
via Getty Images)
President Donald Trump Wednesday morn…

items.title: Ranking Joe Biden’s mistakes
items.summary: This piece critically ranks perceived mistakes from Joe Biden's presidency, with the author defending decisions like his second-term bid and Israel-Palestine policy. It encourages Democrats to move past superficial consensus to genuinely assess the previous administration's substantive failures.

items.title: The narrow path to saving the republic
items.summary:
An American flag waves against a blue sky. (Photo by
Grace Cary
via Getty Images)
Not to be melodramatic, but I really believe the future of American democracy has been hanging in the balance the past few years.
January 6, as it played out, was more farce than tragedy. But normal…

items.title: Adobe acquires image and video enhancement tool maker Topaz Labs
items.summary: Adobe acquired Topaz Labs, a developer of image and video enhancement tools, with plans to integrate their technologies into its suite of applications. This acquisition aims to bolster Adobe's capabilities in media editing.

items.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
items.summary: Google continues to lose top AI researchers, with Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel recently departing for Anthropic, adding to a series of high-profile exits. This ongoing trend suggests a significant brain drain of AI talent from the company to its competitors.

items.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
items.summary: Contrary to predictions of AI eliminating engineering jobs, new data from SignalFire indicates that engineers constitute a growing portion of new hires. This suggests that engineering roles are proving remarkably resilient amidst advancements in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Amazon ups India bet with fresh $13B AI infrastructure investment
items.summary: Amazon announced a new $13 billion investment in AI infrastructure within India, signaling an increased commitment to the region. This strategic move aligns with other global tech companies' efforts to expand their AI capabilities in India.

items.title: Apple raises Mac and iPad prices, spares iPhone for now
items.summary: Apple raises prices of MacBook Air and Pro along with iPad Air and Pro.

items.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
items.summary: Cerebras stock plunged after its first public earnings report, which included a forecast for a narrower gross margin in its core business. The CEO attributed the investor reaction to a misunderstanding of the margin outlook.

items.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
items.summary: Companies are beginning to ration AI budget tokens to prevent employees from excessively using AI tools for minor tasks, ending a brief "tokenmaxxing" period. This indicates a new phase of cost management and controlled AI usage within organizations.

items.title: Elon suffers another day short of trillionaire status
items.summary: Elon Musk remains a multi-hundred-billionaire, as per Bloomberg's Billionaires Index, but has not yet reached trillionaire status. The article humorously points out this continued high wealth, just short of the next major milestone.

items.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
items.summary: Europe is resisting Washington's "chip war" policies, particularly regarding further restrictions on selling older-generation deep ultraviolet chip tools to China. ASML's CEO highlighted that such tools, already a decade old, would be prohibited under the proposed MATCH Act.

items.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
items.summary: Vishal Sikka, ex-Infosys chief, has launched a new startup backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, aiming to disrupt the IT services sector. His new venture brings together experienced professionals from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

items.title: From Fortnite to robots: General Intuition raises $2.3B on bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
items.summary: General Intuition raised $320 million based on its hypothesis that AI agents can develop human-like intuition for real-world tasks by being trained on millions of hours of video game gameplay data. The company aims to scale this AI training approach.

items.title: Google Finance gets a dedicated app for Android
items.summary: Google Finance has introduced a dedicated Android app, offering users watchlists, real-time market data, live financial news, and an AI-powered "Key Moments" feature that explains stock fluctuations. This app aims to centralize financial information for Android users.

items.title: Netris raises $15M Series A from a16z to help AI neoclouds go live faster
items.summary: Netris secured $15 million in Series A funding from a16z to develop software for network switches, enabling AI neocloud operators to deploy faster. This platform aims to streamline the process of bringing new AI cloud infrastructures online.

items.title: New website names and shames companies that still don’t offer passkeys to users
items.summary: A new website is publicly shaming major companies that have not yet implemented passkeys, a highly secure login method. It reports that nearly a quarter of the world's most popular websites still lack support for this security feature.

items.title: Parker Conrad knows which employees are worth their AI spend and says Rippling can help you, too
items.summary: Parker Conrad emphasized the importance of assessing the return on investment for employee AI tool usage, citing examples of high individual annual spending on AI. His company, Rippling, aims to assist businesses in tracking and optimizing these AI expenditures.

items.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
items.summary: A US company experienced a quadruple increase in revenue to $41.45 billion and a profit surge to $28.2 billion year-over-year, largely benefiting from the current memory chip shortage. This highlights a significant financial gain during the chip crunch.

items.title: Trump admin bars Polestar from selling its new EVs in the US
items.summary: The Department of Commerce declined to give the Chinese-owned automaker a special authorization to keep selling EVs in the U.S.

items.title: Trump admin proposes axing brake-pedal requirement for AVs in a boost for Tesla
items.summary: The Trump administration's Department of Transportation proposed removing the brake-pedal requirement for autonomous vehicles, a change that could significantly benefit companies like Tesla. This regulation shift applies to vehicles designed solely for automated driving systems.

items.title: Douglas Murray: The Iranian Regime Means What It Says
items.summary: Douglas Murray provides an analysis on the intentions of the Iranian regime, alongside a discussion of the current political state in Britain following Keir Starmer's resignation. He offers a clear-eyed perspective on threats to Western civilization.

items.title: Great Americans: The Strange Dependence of an Independent Woman
items.summary: Eliza Lucas Pinckney (1722–1793), an 18th-century woman who independently managed plantations and pioneered indigo production in South Carolina, exemplifies a paradox of self-reliance. Her life story highlights that even the most independent individuals operate within a web of dependencies, challenging conventional notions of self-sufficiency.

items.title: New York’s New Squad
items.summary:
On Tuesday, the Democratic establishment suffered a crushing defeat when
nine out of 10 candidates
endorsed by the local chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) coasted to victory in their primaries. In some instances, long-serving progressives were caught in the cr…

items.title: WATCH: Mamdani’s Allies Just Swept New York. What Comes Next?
items.summary:
On Tuesday night, the democratic socialists swept New York.
In a series of closely watched congressional primaries, all three candidates endorsed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani defeated more moderate rivals, including two incumbents. Their victories, which in heavily blue districts all…

items.title: ‘Freeze the Rent’ Is Really About Rage and Revolution
items.summary: New York City's Rent Guidelines Board will decide on Mayor Zohran Mamdani's controversial "freeze the rent" promise for rent-stabilized apartments, following raucous public hearings. Unlike other cities with formulaic rent controls, NYC's process is marked by intense activism and public disruption.

items.title: Epictetus on Love and Loss: The Stoic Strategy for Surviving Heartbreak
items.summary: Epictetus's Stoic philosophy provides a strategy for enduring heartbreak by embracing the transient nature of all things, including love. He proposes regarding cherished relationships as temporarily "on loan" from the universe, which can temper grief when loss inevitably occurs.

items.title: BYOK is my new go-to distraction-free writing tool
items.summary: The BYOK, a $199 minimalist device, offers a low-resolution LCD screen for distraction-free writing, allowing only text editing. This "Bring Your Own Keyboard" tool is designed to enhance focus by removing other functionalities.

items.title: EverQuest Legends is a powerful nostalgia machine
items.summary: This article reflects on the nostalgic power of EverQuest Legends, intertwined with a personal narrative about the author's estranged father and the process of saying goodbye. It explores how digital spaces can evoke powerful memories and emotions connected to real-life relationships and loss.

items.title: Leica’s $6,690 SL3-P pairs 44-megapixel stills with 8K video
items.summary: Leica unveiled its new $6,690 SL3-P camera, which features a 44-megapixel sensor and 8K video capabilities. This model builds upon previous SL3 versions by integrating a new hybrid autofocus system for enhanced performance.

items.title: Oppo’s Bubble selfie screen is crying out for Qi2
items.summary: Oppo's Bubble is a small, wireless second screen that serves as a selfie monitor or camera remote, yet its design lacks Qi2 magnetic attachment. The author notes this absence highlights a missed opportunity for Android OEMs to integrate Qi2 for improved accessory functionality.

items.title: Slate’s electric truck: all the news about the ultra-minimal EV
items.summary: Slate Auto is a new startup that emerged out of a secretive project called “Re:Car” within Re:Build Manufacturing, a domestic manufacturing project backed by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos. The company’s first electric vehicle is a barebones electric pickup that’s roughly a third of t…
